name: parse_groks
description:
Parses the `value` using multiple [`grok`](https://github.com/daschl/grok/tree/master/patterns) patterns. All patterns [listed here](https://github.com/daschl/grok/tree/master/patterns)
are supported.

arguments.name: patterns
arguments.description: The [Grok patterns](https://github.com/daschl/grok/tree/master/patterns), which are tried in order until the first match.

arguments.name: aliases
arguments.description: The shared set of grok aliases that can be referenced in the patterns to simplify them.

arguments.name: alias_sources
arguments.description: Path to the file containing aliases in a JSON format.

internal_failure_reasons: `value` fails to parse using the provided `pattern`.
`alias_sources` is not a string or doesn't point to a valid file.

examples.title: Parse using multiple Grok patterns
examples.source:
parse_groks!(
	"2020-10-02T23:22:12.223222Z info Hello world",
	patterns: [
		"%{common_prefix} %{_status} %{_message}",
		"%{common_prefix} %{_message}",
	],
	aliases: {
		"common_prefix": "%{_timestamp} %{_loglevel}",
		"_timestamp": "%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:timestamp}",
		"_loglevel": "%{LOGLEVEL:level}",
		"_status": "%{POSINT:status}",
		"_message": "%{GREEDYDATA:message}"
	}
)

notices:
We recommend using community-maintained Grok patterns when possible, as they're more likely to be properly
vetted and improved over time than bespoke patterns.
